- High-value criteria emphasize scaling signals like tech expansions or restructuring, tying to ROI from data models and integration.
- Disqualifiers are limited to explicit mismatches like non-insurance operations, as site/Context show broad commercial applicability.
- Research sources prioritize scalable public indicators like job postings for underwriting roles or news on digital initiatives.

ICP Fit Criteria
- Companies operating in insurance brokerage or carriers with portfolios of 10+ product types (e.g., commercial lines for restaurants, wholesalers), indicating need for product organization efficiency.
- Midwest-based or Chinese-owned businesses handling specialized assets like property/food distribution, as evidenced by public client testimonials or "About" descriptions.
- Firms mentioning manual processes for premiums/risks/conditions in annual reports, blogs, or case studies, signaling baseline alignment with Anvo's data modeling.
- Operations involving policy administration for multiple segments (e.g., hospitality/tech), where flexibility in analytics is implied by business scale.

High-Value Fit Criteria
- Recent press releases or news sections announcing digital transformation in policy management/underwriting, suggesting urgent ROI from reliable data models.
- Active job postings for roles like "insurance product configurator," "underwriting rules specialist," or "premium calculation analyst," indicating scaling pains.

Disqualification Criteria
- Companies exclusively in non-commercial lines (e.g., pure personal auto/health without brokerage elements), conflicting with commercial focus.
- Public statements prioritizing in-house custom builds for all product modeling, negating external efficiency tools.

OBR operates under the leadership of CEO Stan Hays and COO/CFO Will Cleaver Jr. Since its inception, OBR has served over 13 million meals and has deployed for nearly 4,700 days.

OBR's core services include disaster relief, food distribution, and year-round hunger relief programs. Short Description:
Bethel Neighborhood Center opened its doors on April 11, 1911, as a community center of The Women's Baptist Home Mission Society. They primarily served needy families living near or working in the meat-packing plants of the West Bottoms of Kansas City. Many of them were new immigrants from Eastern European countries. Bethel has been located in the urban core of Kansas City, Kansas in the heart of one of the ten poorest counties in the nation per capita. This area has a high concentration for immigrant resettlement in the Greater Kansas City metropolitan area.

Our neighborhood has gone through many changes over the last 111 years, but our mission remains the same. Over the decades, several waves of immigrants from Africa, South and Central America, and Asia have come to Kansas City, Kansas and Bethel Center has been supporting them in their assimilation to the American life and culture.

Our mission is to empower individuals to achieve a renewed sense of hope, wellness, and self-sufficiency. 

The Center serves more than 10,000 people annually. In addition to after-school and summer programs for neighborhood children/youth, the Center offers a senior lunch program, a food pantry, ESL/Citizenship classes, healthy lifestyles classes, Christmas gifts, a community garden, sewing training, and provides advocacy services on behalf of the poor and the immigrants. With COVID, Bethel's role as a trusted advocate with the populations Bethel serves has become even more recognized and important. Bethel finds itself at the forefront of community efforts to combat this health crisis. The complexities of the pandemic and the way it has been communicated in the media has been especially challenging for those new to the US. Bethel has been called on even more by various health partners in the community to serve as a trusted advocate. Services are often available, but people don't know how to access the services or if they should access them. Bethel serves as an important bridge.

Short Description:
The roots of V's Italiano Ristorante date back to 1963 when "V" (Vita) and Jay Totta decided to open the first "V's Café", consisting of a seven stool counter, three tables, and four booths, for a total seating capacity of 35 people. Utilizing family recipes rich in tradition, high quality standards, and the personal touch of "V" herself greeting customers, an ever expanding business was created.

In 1966, the Totta's acquired and remodeled a building that seemed massive at the time. It could accommodate 85 guests; nearly two and one-half times the original seating. However, that building was soon outgrown. "V" and her husband Jay, an architect, dreamed of someday being able to design and build their own building that would create an atmosphere of elegant, old world charm.

The dream became reality in 1972 when the present building was completed. The dining room comfortably accommodates 150 patrons in a relaxed atmosphere. The building also has a beautiful lounge and three private banquet rooms available for groups of up to 135 guests. On the exterior of the building is a beautiful wood arbor with grape vines planted by V's father, Tony Barber. These vines are now carefully tended by the fourth generation, and can be viewed from the interior of the "Vista Vite" room. You may also enjoy a cocktail under the vines, while listening to the cascading waters of our beautiful stone waterfall in the intimate setting of our "Garden Patio".

Short Description:
Kansas Appleseed is a statewide 501(c)(3) nonprofit, nonpartisan advocacy organization dedicated to the belief that Kansans, working together, can build a state full of thriving, inclusive, and just communities. We're working for a future where:

-All Kansans have the resources they need to support themselves and raise healthy families.
-All Kansans can participate fully in the community under equal protection of the law.
-All Kansans benefit from a fair and effective judicial system.

Short Description:
We pride ourselves in creating a traditional authentic Tapas Bar atmosphere. This includes our warm decor and the traditional hanging of Serrano hams and dried bacalaos. Our service reflects a traditional tapas experience as you might find in Madrid, Barcelona or Sevilla.
 
Tapas style service means that food is prepared to order and delivered to the table plates at a time. We do not intend on delivering all the food in traditional course style, or the entire table at once. This way you will be able to enjoy an authentic tapas evening that encourages sharing of plates, lots of gossip and sangria.
 
WHAT IS TAPAS?
 The word ‘tapas' literally means ‘lid,' and the origin of the tapas custom most probably lies in the placing of a small plate or lid over a glass of wine when served in the neighborhood bodegas.It is also said that a centuries-old decree insisted all bars and roadhouses serve food as an accompaniment to wine, in an attempt to ensure at least a modicum of sobriety among the nation's coach drivers.
 
A tapa is a delicious morsel of food that defines a lifestyle as well as a culinary style. Tapas in Spain are almost always accompanied by wine, but they are as much about talking as they are about eating and drinking. The wine is, perhaps, the medium that holds the conversation, the friends, and the food together. The primary purpose of tapas is to talk to friends, to share the gossip of the day.

We hope you enjoy your evening and please come back to see us again soon.

Prospection Solutions is a global leader in advanced foreign material detection and comprehensive product and packaging inspection systems for the food industry. Our commitment to elevating food safety standards drives us to develop cutting-edge technology and automation that enhance quality, optimize labor, and facilitate immediate process improvements.

Focused on innovation, we partner with companies to deliver tailored solutions that ensure consistent quality and free up manpower for critical tasks. Our award-winning Low-Density Foreign Material Detection System, an industry benchmark, scans products at impressive rates.

Our technology portfolio includes:
    High-Speed Product and Package Inspection Systems: Maximizes throughput while ensuring food safety, with advanced detection capabilities that significantly reduce contamination risk.
    Nugget and Patty Inspection: Sets new standards for low-density foreign material detection, ensuring top quality and safety.

    X-Ray + Optical Seal Inspection: Monitors seals and inspects for foreign objects, weight, shape, and defects.
    Metal Detection Series: Multi-frequency with automatic balancing for consistent accuracy.

At Prospection Solutions, our mission is to partner with clients in implementing innovative inspection solutions that reduce labor demands, optimize critical operations, and improve food safety standards at every stage of production. Our systems are designed for seamless integration with upstream and downstream equipment, ensuring an efficient and cohesive production process.

Short Description:
Kauffman Scholars, Inc. (KSI) is a non-profit organization based in Kansas City, Missouri, established in 2003 by the Ewing Marion Kauffman Foundation. The organization focused on supporting urban, low-income students from seventh grade through college completion, particularly those from historically marginalized racial and ethnic groups. Over its 20-year run, KSI impacted more than 2,500 students, emphasizing postsecondary success to enhance career opportunities in the Kansas City metro area.

KSI provided a comprehensive support system that included college scholarships, academic tutoring, college preparation, and mentoring. The program also addressed personal challenges such as housing and food insecurity, maintaining long-term relationships with students throughout their college journey. Evaluation results indicated that 90% of program completers attended college or postsecondary training, with 54% earning a degree or credential. KSI's efforts significantly contributed to college access and success for its scholars, benefiting individual students, families, and the wider community.

Short Description:
Tom's Town Distilling Co. draws its name and inspiration from the country's most polarizing and corrupt political boss, Tom Pendergast. Pendergast had roots in the liquor business as a saloon keeper and as the founder of a wholesale liquor company.

Under Pendergast's influence, Kansas City flouted Prohibition. Tom's irreverence for federal statutes in favor of bare-knuckled local business dealings allowed his town to emerge as the "Paris of the Plains." Money, jazz, and spirits flowed like water, right out in the open. When a newspaperman asked Pendergast how he justified ignoring Prohibition, he shrugged and said,  "The people are thirsty."

Today the people are still thirsty in Kansas City and across the U.S. We aim to satisfy that thirst by providing spirits in the time-honored ways that produce superior products. You can taste our high standards and attention to detail in every sip of the gin, vodka, and bourbon we make on Main Street in downtown Kansas City.

Short Description:
The mission of Kansas City Community Gardens is to assist low-income households and community groups in the Kansas City metropolitan area to produce vegetables and fruit from garden plots located in backyards, vacant lots, and at community sites.

KCCG helps over18,000 individuals in the Kansas City area each year. The organization maintains or partners with over 500 community garden sites. 

KCCG provides free gardening workshops, technical assistance, garden supplies, and other resources to individuals, families, neighborhood organizations, nonprofit agencies, and other groups throughout the metropolitan Kansas City area through the Self-Help Gardening and Community Partner Gardens programs. In addition, KCCG's Schoolyard Gardens program staff help schools to create and grow gardens to improve students' knowledge about nutrition and the importance of fresh fruits and vegetables to a healthy diet. KCCG's Beanstalk Children's Garden complements the efforts of Schoolyard Gardens staff by allowing school and community groups, families, and other visitors to learn about plant science, food production, nutrition, and insects during summer tours of this unique garden. Short Description:
Askinosie Chocolate is a small-batch, bean-to-bar chocolate manufacturer based in Springfield, Missouri. Founded in 2007 by Shawn Askinosie, the company is known for its direct trade practices and ethical sourcing from smallholder farmers. Askinosie emphasizes a mission that includes producing high-quality chocolate, sharing profits with cocoa farmers, and educating local students through initiatives like Chocolate University.

The company specializes in single-origin chocolate bars made from 100% traceable cocoa beans sourced from regions such as Ecuador, the Philippines, and Tanzania. Their product lineup features dark chocolate, dark milk chocolate, white chocolate, all-natural cocoa powder, roasted cocoa nibs, and chocolate hazelnut spread. Askinosie Chocolate is committed to sustainability and community empowerment, building personal relationships with farmers and promoting educational programs for youth. The company has received recognition for its contributions to the craft chocolate movement and maintains a small, dedicated team focused on quality and social impact.

We're a 4th generation family-owned company providing three services to the KC metro area:  Pest Control, Lawn Care and Fire Safety.  This year marks our 65th anniversary of providing Pest Control services to Kansas Citians!  

We became well known by the KC community with our green and white Volkswagen Beetles our technicians started driving in 1990 alongside the motto: "The only bugs you'll want at your home will be ours!" Short Description:
Looking for a public relations agency? Look East.

In today's food system, the success of your business depends on whether or not consumers and other stakeholders trust you. That's why we focus on helping your business or organization grow trust, so you can keep your social license and maintain your freedom to operate.

How do we go about growing trust?

Our peer-reviewed and published trust model shows that trust is based on three essential elements - influential others, competence and confidence. Influential others include family, friends and credentialed experts. Competence is the science and information that validates what you do. Confidence involves showing your customers and stakeholders that you share their values, and it's the most important factor of all. In fact, our research shows that shared values are 3 to 5 times more important in growing consumer trust than just providing information.

Shared values are what connect us. They help us realize that we often care about the same things, even when we view issues differently.

We're here to find, communicate and connect based on shared values - the foundation for building trust. As a full-service public relations agency, we'll walk with you each step of the process, from developing insights to implementing a strategy.

Our multi-disciplined approach means we can work in any sector of food and agriculture. Our extensive network across the food system enables us to understand the broader perspective from farm to processor to consumer.

Short Description:
Redemptorist Social Services Center continues to be the heart of mid-town Kansas City! 2016 marked the Center's 30th year of providing a lifeline to individuals facing chronic poverty, the working poor, the elderly, the home-bound and the homeless, the newly and the long term unemployed. Our comprehensive programs of Emergency Client Assistance, Senior Services, Legal Assistance and Health Maintenance provide over 1,900 critical client assists each month with shelter, utility and medical needs, food, clothing and transportation, education and work related needs. We convey our sincerest gratitude and thanks to the individual donors, churches, businesses and grant foundations whose financial support has been instrumental in our growth and success. You made it all possible and we couldn't have done it without you!

The caring, competent and knowledgeable staff personally works one on one with clients to help with the client's current crisis and uncover contributing factors. Center staff and volunteers carry out their mission of serving the poor and elderly with a passion that is unsurpassed. Their reputation in the non-profit community of Kansas City is one of respect and admiration. Our mission is to prevent a temporary crisis from becoming a permanent tragedy. As a result, the Center is continually evolving to meet the changing needs of their clients and the community. The result of this commitment by the Board, volunteers and staff allows the Center to operate with a high degree of efficiency. As the Center continues its commitment to serve, we will add new programs and services based on the need. Our close ties to the community have served the Center well as we seek support for our projects that provide stability and hope to the people of metropolitan Kansas City.

Short Description:
At SES, our goal is to help clients become more sustainable and safer. We take on challenges like managing foreign animal disease outbreaks, resource conservation, and climate change. These are some of the biggest challenges that face our public and private clients – and our world. 

Since 1998, we've worked to provide actionable, measurable outcomes that deliver on our clients' goals, including:

Verification
Ensure your sustainability and environmental programs are meeting goals with independent verification. SES is a trusted carbon offset verifier for national voluntary programs and regulatory programs including California's LCFS standards.

Sustainability and Climate Consulting
Meet your sustainability commitments and climate goals with GHG accounting, reporting, and goal-based program development.

Emergency Management
Protect your organization with emergency management planning, training, and exercises. Build resilience and preparedness for all types of emergencies including foreign animal disease.

Program Development
Build end-to-end, metrics-backed sustainability and compliance programs that include planning, implementation, maintenance, and auditing.

Natural Resource Management
Manage, conserve, and sustain croplands, rangeland soils, plants, water, and wildlife. Assess environmental impacts of planned projects, including nature-based solutions to climate change.

Environmental Management
Get expert support for environmental reporting, compliance, permitting, assessment, investigation, and restoration projects.

Organic Integrity and Food Safety
We build Organic Fraud Prevention Plans and HACCP Plans. We help companies with supplier outreach, education, verification and sustainability programs, and organic compliance. Short Description:
The original plant-based superfoods company. Our farm grew the first cereal grass in 1932, used for the phenomenal research in books about wheatgrass, such as those by Ann Wigmore. The studies used dried whole-food cereal grass from our farm. The results were so significant that the American Medical Association certified it as a nutritious source of dark green, leafy vegetables in 1939.
 
Pines is an organic farmer producing green superfoods for the Natural Products Industry. The Pines mission is to promote organic farming through sustainable communities and to provide healthy food.

With a legacy of sustainable farming going back to the 1930s, Pines continues to fulfill this mission. On our original farm in Lawrence, KS, we grow the highest-quality wheatgrass, barley grass, alfalfa leaf, oat grass, rye, kale, and other nutrient-dense foods. We supply these as raw materials for manufacturers that create nutritional supplements and food products. We also offer a line of green products that retailers sell nationwide. Our consumer products are packaged in glass jars only, reducing plastic consumption.

In keeping with our mission, Pines has donated millions of servings of our cereal grasses to feeding programs worldwide. Through our Foundation, we make annual cash contributions to feeding programs and other charitable organizations. Our donations go towards feeding the poor and hungry, schools and educational programs, supporting natural resources, and improving nutrition worldwide.

Pines is the original home and research facility of Cerophyl Labs, founded in the 1930s. Cerophyl was founded by Dr. Charles Schnabel, known as "the father of wheatgrass."  The history of wheatgrass and other green superfoods goes back more than 80 years to Dr. Schnabel and other scientists. Those pioneers operated out of the exact location now used by Pines International. Pines International still uses its laboratories and the same farm north of Lawrence, Kansas.

What started as a dream for Emmett O'Rear in 1977 of bringing him, his wife, Mary, and their daughters home to start a family business of pressing apples into cider has now turned into a 3rd generation family-owned operation. We make apple cider and produce nine flavors of our small batch Lost Trail craft soda, sparkling ciders, and of course, our apple cider donuts. Although our love for apples could not stop at producing sweet cider, in the last year, we have branched out and started making hard cider!

You might see our apple cider in your local grocer during the fall months. This is when you can visit our facility and observe our apple press in action as we press 50+ tons of apples daily, the same barn that started pressing apples over 45 years ago. You can shop at our Country Store, where you can purchase all of our products, from the fresh pressed cider to the Honeycrisp BBQ Sauce made with our apple cider, open year-round.  

Not only do we produce beverages and have a retail store, but we also have a Family Farm that is open from mid-September through October that hosts a 10-acre pumpkin patch and corn maze, wagon rides, and tons of family-friendly activities.

Short Description:
Cross-Lines was established in 1963 as "a place of hope" for those struggling with the hardships of poverty. We provide direct assistance to at-risk families, the elderly and single individuals struggling to make ends meet. Our mission is to provide people in the Kansas City area affected by poverty with services and opportunities that encourage self-confidence, meet the needs of today, and provide tools for future self-sufficiency.   

Cross-Lines has three main programs:
Housing Stabilization--Cross-Lines Housing Stabilization Program promotes housing and family stability by providing rent and utility assistance as well as homeless re-housing services. Many low-income families are one paycheck away from a financial crisis. 
Our Case Managers work with people to address the immediate needs and also help the families find long-term financial stability
to keep families together in their homes.

Hunger Relief--Cross-Lines Hunger Relief Program provides nutritious foods and alleviates hunger for those in need. Cross-Lines has three primary hunger relief services: our Community Kitchen, Food Pantry and Commodities program.

Basic Needs--Cross-Lines Basic Needs Program provides for needs that many of us take for granted. We provide services that ensure the dignity and respect of the individuals and families in our community. Everyone deserves to be clean and healthy. Cross-Lines provides toiletries, cleaning products, diapers, medicine and dental assistance, clothing, and laundry and shower services.

Cross-Lines is supported by a network of local businesses, individuals, faith communities, foundations and civic organizations. We provide daily direct assistance to at-risk families, the elderly, and single individuals living in poverty. 

Cross-Lines couldn't exist without our amazing volunteers!  We are dependent on the more than 1,200 people who share their time, talent and hearts with us each year.

Short Description:
Lawrence Beer Company is a Brewery in Lawrence, KS that also has a full service kitchen.

Like most people these days, we've visited our fair share of breweries.  Some were big and fancy, some were small and just getting started, but our favorites all had similar characteristics.  We love when we can sit on a patio in the sun drinking a few beers with friends and just watch the neighborhood roll in.  Pushing strollers, riding bikes, stopping quickly for a thirst quencher while out for a jog, really using the brewery as a community gathering spot like they were always meant to be.  We're not looking for world domination here we just want to make quality, fresh beer and food for the community that we live in, and then share it with the people who live there.  We want you to come in and hang out with us. 

Staying community based rather than distributing large scale frees us up to constantly play around with different styles and small batch releases, and ensures the beer you're drinking is as fresh as possible. 

We've already had unbelievable support from our families, friends, and our city, and we want to make sure we create something that everyone can be proud of and feel like they had a part in making great.  Good beer, good food, and good camaraderie, with a few shenanigans mixed in as well.

Short Description:
Smith Electric Vehicles, founded in 1920 in Newcastle, England, specialized in manufacturing zero-emission commercial electric vehicles, particularly electric trucks. The company relaunched in 2007 and incorporated in the US in 2009, moving its headquarters to Kansas City, Missouri, in 2011. Smith produced over 800 units of electric trucks from 2010 to 2015, offering a range of vehicles with gross weights from 3,500 to 12,000 kilograms. 

Smith's notable innovations included the SmithLink telematics system for real-time battery and location data and the proprietary Smith Drive system for power management. Their electric trucks were designed for various industries, including food and beverage, utility, telecommunications, and military applications. Key models like the Newton truck featured a top speed of 60 mph and a range of up to 150 miles per charge. Smith served major clients such as PepsiCo, FedEx, and the U.S. Military, achieving significant market presence in the UK electric vehicle sector.

J. Rieger & Co. is a Kansas City-based distillery with a rich history dating back to 1887. Originally founded by Jacob Rieger, the distillery was the largest mail-order whiskey house in the U.S. before closing in 1919 due to Prohibition. It was revived in 2014 by Andy Rieger and Ryan Maybee, becoming the first legal distillery in Kansas City since that era. The distillery operates from a historic facility in the East Bottoms' Electric Park District, producing award-winning spirits and offering a variety of experiences.

The company specializes in premium spirits, including Kansas City Whiskey, Monogram Whiskey, and Premium Wheat Vodka. J. Rieger & Co. emphasizes craftsmanship and provides guided distillery tours that cover the history and production processes of their spirits. They also host events and offer innovative cocktail recipes, enhancing the guest experience at their landmark facility. Their products are available nationwide through various partners and are featured in local bars and restaurants.

Short Description:
Bissinger's Handcrafted Chocolatier is a family-owned luxury chocolate brand with a rich history dating back to 1668. Originally the official confectioner to King Louis XIV in Paris, the company is now based in St. Louis, Missouri. Bissinger's specializes in small-batch, handcrafted chocolates made using traditional techniques and fresh, ethically sourced ingredients, including sustainably harvested cocoa, fruits, and nuts.

The brand offers a variety of premium chocolates, including truffles, bars, and seasonal treats like Pumpkin Pie Bark. Their products are crafted from timeless recipes found in the Bissinger family's 1899 cookbook, with modern adaptations such as plant-based options. Bissinger's emphasizes artisan production in copper kettles to ensure quality and flavor consistency. The company also engages in community initiatives and is expanding its operations with plans for a new factory, café, and luxury packaging by 2025. Products are available nationwide through over 1,000 specialty stores, as well as their own boutiques and online platform.

Short Description:
Trust. The word is often misplaced, particularly in the moving industry. Nonetheless, it is the cornerstone of what Great Day Moving is built upon.  Though relatively new to the world of moving and franchise development, owners Roger and Dana Ward have spent a lifetime preparing for this opportunity to lead a company built on the foundation of service  and innovation. 

To understand the business of Great Day Moving, one must understand the Wards. They are servants first, striving to model their faith through actions and relationships.  They believe that a move is never 'just a move'; it is a transition that means something different to each client, and they treat each interaction like the precious thing that it is: unique, personal and vitally important.

To Great Day Moving, trust is more than just a word; it is a combination of expert service and personal touch. It is more than moving boxes. It is the understanding that whatever they are moving is more than just 'stuff'; it is the stuff that life is made of.  While their movers have a reputation for actually making moving day fun, the understanding that they play an integral part in what is often one of a client's most stressful experiences is never taken lightly.

Stewards at heart, Roger and Dana Ward understand that the business they are building is about more than the bottom line; it is about empowering others, serving the greater good and providing a structure for those who aspire to follow in their footsteps.

The philosophy of Great Day Moving reflects how the Wards prefer to be treated; they value honesty, accountability and personal service. They walk the delicate line between running a business and cultivating friendships, and often people start as the former but remain as the latter.  They work to affect change every day, and to restore faith to an industry that has been tarnished. They believe that under the flaws lies an industry that can not only be restored, but structured and redefined.

The Department of Grain and Food Science at Kansas State University (K-State) is a leading academic department in Manhattan, Kansas, specializing in grains, food, and feed science. With over 110 years of experience, it is the only U.S. institution offering undergraduate degrees in areas such as feed science and management, milling science and management, and bakery science and management. The department also provides M.S. and Ph.D. degrees in grain science, focusing on high-quality education and innovative research.

K-State's department emphasizes strong industry partnerships and offers four unique undergraduate majors, all designed to prepare students for successful careers in their respective fields. The department's research covers various aspects of food and feed safety, milling, baking, and livestock nutrition, supported by state-of-the-art facilities like the O.H. Kruse Feed Technology Innovation Center. Through its initiatives, the department contributes to K-State's vision as a top public research university, fostering collaborations that enhance food safety and sustainability.

Short Description:
At Westchester Village of Lenexa, our staff has a philosophy of care that is implemented into the every day operations. Our philosophy is to include dignity, quality, choices, respect and community into what we provide every day. We always strive to fulfill our vision using the value statement as a guide. We are a CCRC and offer four levels of living:

Independent Living
Residential independent living offers the privacy of your own home without the troubles of its care and maintenance. Our independent living residences feature expansive, light-filled floor plans with full kitchens and modern conveniences.

Assisted Living
In addition to amenities included in Residential Independent Living, services available for Assisted Living include:

- Assistance with bathing, dressing, and medication
- Three restaurant-style meals daily
- Wellness checks and a care plan supervised by a registered nurse
- And much more!

Long-Term Care
For the resident, everything we do revolves around the resident and his or her personal well-being. Each and every resident receives the attention of a highly trained team that develops an individualized goal-oriented program designed to foster personal independence and maximize quality of life.

Rehabilitation
After a full assessment by our Health Care Team, we will prepare a resident centered care plan and do everything we can to support your health and well-being during your stay at Westchester Village. Getting to know the person before treating the illness enhances rehabilitation and increases the positive impact on their recovery.

Short Description:
Reconciliation Services is a nonprofit organization based in Kansas City, Missouri, founded in 1987. It focuses on addressing economic and racial disparities, poverty, and trauma in the urban core, particularly east of Troost Avenue. Originally established as Reconciliation Ministries by Fr. David Altschul and Thelma Altschul, the organization aims to transform Troost from a dividing line into a gathering place that fosters community strength through dignity, community, and advocacy.

The organization offers three main initiatives: REVEAL Social and Mental Health Services, which provides therapy and case management; Thelma’s Kitchen, a pay-it-forward café that serves thousands of meals; and the Foster Grandparents Program, which engages seniors in mentoring roles. In 2024, Reconciliation Services is set to serve over 3,500 clients, emphasizing racial equity and community engagement through various programs and events. The organization continues to evolve with facility expansions and community-focused initiatives to enhance its impact.

Short Description:
Kansas Wheat is the cooperative agreement between the Kansas Wheat Commission and the Kansas Association of Wheat Growers, joining together as "leaders in the adoption of profitable innovations for wheat."

The Kansas Wheat Commission is a grower-funded, grower-governed advocacy organization working to secure the future of Kansas wheat in the global market through international trade, research, export system studies and continually improved varieties of wheat. Its mission is to increase wheat producer productivity and profitability through research, education and domestic and international market development. The KWC is funded by a voluntary, two cent assessment on each bushel of wheat produced in Kansas.

The Kansas Association of Wheat Growers is a member-governed organization representing wheat growers in planning legislation, advising government and international agencies on matters affecting wheat and providing grass-roots leadership to the U.S. wheat industry.

Short Description:
Balanced is a public health and nutrition advocacy organization campaigning for healthier menus in schools, hospitals, and other critical institutions. 

We believe everyday people, communities, and organizations like ours have the power to transform the food system by encouraging institutions to improve the healthfulness of their menus and demanding change from the food industry. 

Unlike other organizations with similar goals, our focus is on systems change - not individual education.

Balanced believes most people want to improve their lives by replacing the unhealthy foods in their diets, but because of the way our food system is designed and controlled by a number of major food companies, healthy eating is often impossible.

We believe there are victims of our food system for whom only systems-level change will have an impact - people who have limited or no power over their food choices at all.  Education alone is not sufficient for students with limited lunch options, patients in hospitals selecting from premade menus, or employees dependent on worksite cafeterias.

For those people, and others, food environments have to be healthier. And that will require major improvements to our food system.

Our advocacy work is rooted in leading nutrition science and the recommendations made by world-renowned healthcare organizations.

We believe the food system and our community institutions have to support increased consumption of fruits, vegetables, plant-proteins, and whole grains and decreased consumption of disease-causing meat and egg products.

Our agenda is simple: change menus, save lives.

Short Description:
Kanbe's Markets is a nonprofit organization based in Kansas City, Missouri, founded in 2017. Its mission is to create a more inclusive food system by addressing food insecurity and promoting food justice, particularly in underserved neighborhoods. The organization operates a zero-waste model that rescues unsellable fresh produce from wholesalers and redistributes it to communities in need, focusing on Black neighborhoods and food deserts.

The organization partners with local small businesses to establish "Healthy Corner Stores," which provide affordable fresh produce at prices lower than conventional grocery stores. Kanbe's also offers home delivery services for those unable to visit stores and engages volunteers in sorting rescued food for various distributions. Since its inception, Kanbe's has diverted millions of pounds of food from landfills and has significantly impacted local economies by enhancing small business profitability and ensuring access to healthy food options.
